boards.display_name: DFRobot Beetle ESP32-C6 (DFR1117) — coin-sized board around the ESP32-C6-FH4 (single-core 160 MHz RISC-V, WiFi 6, BLE 5
boards.name: esp32-c6-dfr1117
boards.description: DFRobot Beetle ESP32-C6 (DFR1117) — coin-sized board around the ESP32-C6-FH4 (single-core 160 MHz RISC-V, WiFi 6, BLE 5.0, 802.15.4). 4 MB flash, no PSRAM. Native USB-Serial-JTAG over USB-C (flash + console on one cable). On-board single-colour user LED at GPIO15 and a TP4057 LiPo charger. r2-workshop's RISC-V-family reference carrier (peer to the Xtensa esp32-s3 devkitc / xiao carriers).

boards.display_name: Espressif ESP32-S3-DevKitC-1 — Espressif's reference development board for the ESP32-S3 family
boards.name: esp32-s3-devkitc
boards.description: Espressif ESP32-S3-DevKitC-1 — Espressif's reference development board for the ESP32-S3 family. Xtensa LX7 dual-core (240 MHz), WiFi + BLE 5. Reference variant is the N8R8: 8 MB flash + 8 MB octal PSRAM. Two USB ports: native USB-OTG (USB-Serial-JTAG console + flash) and a CP2102 UART. On-board addressable RGB LED (WS2812) on GPIO38 (v1.1) or GPIO48 (v1.0). 45 broken-out GPIOs — the high-GPIO-count Xtensa carrier in r2-workshop, current default per ADR-002.

boards.display_name: DFRobot LoRaWAN ESP32-S3 Dev Board (DFR1195) — ESP32-S3-WROOM-1-N4 (Xtensa LX7 dual-core @ 240 MHz, WiFi 4 + BLE 5) plus a Semtech SX1262 LoRa radio (direct LoRa P2P / one-to-many / LoRaWAN / Meshtastic), a 0
boards.name: esp32-s3-dfr1195
boards.description: DFRobot LoRaWAN ESP32-S3 Dev Board (DFR1195) — ESP32-S3-WROOM-1-N4 (Xtensa LX7 dual-core @ 240 MHz, WiFi 4 + BLE 5) plus a Semtech SX1262 LoRa radio (direct LoRa P2P / one-to-many / LoRaWAN / Meshtastic), a 0.96" 160×80 colour TFT (ST7735-family, SPI), two user buttons (Key1 on GPIO18, Key2 on the GPIO0 boot strap), an on-board LED (GPIO21) and a battery-sense ADC (GPIO1). USB-C with the ESP32-S3 native USB-Serial-JTAG. The first tri-radio (WiFi + BLE + LoRa) carrier in the catalogue — the Phase-3 transient-networking hardware test node.

boards.display_name: Seeed Studio XIAO ESP32-S3 (Pre-Soldered SKU) — coin-sized 21 × 17
boards.name: esp32-s3-xiao
boards.description: Seeed Studio XIAO ESP32-S3 (Pre-Soldered SKU) — coin-sized 21 × 17.5 mm ESP32-S3 board around the ESP32-S3R8 die (Xtensa LX7 dual-core, 8 MB flash + 8 MB octal PSRAM). USB-C with native USB-Serial-JTAG. On-board LiPo charger + buck regulator with BAT+/BAT− solder pads on the back (no JST-PH). Only 11 broken-out GPIOs (D0–D10). FSM status LED is the on-board mono yellow LED on GPIO21 (LEDC PWM driven) — same mono-LED pattern as the dfr1117 carrier, no external WS2812 required. r2-workshop's alternative Xtensa carrier (ADR-001 promoted it briefly, ADR-002 reverted default to DevKitC); fully supported.

ensembles.name: rocker-sensor
ensembles.description: Sensor role-ensemble: the ESP32 sample pipeline on each rig device. Reads calibrated triaxial acceleration, writes it to an SD ring, streams it to the controller, and owns the on-device capture state machine + event-mark sidecar. Deployment-specific in exactly two places — the sensing plugin and the domain (accelerometer) sentant.

plugins.name: data-tcp
plugins.description: TCP server on port 21047 implementing LIST / GET / DEL / DEL_ALL over the captures directory on the SD card. Used by the dashboard to download named capture files and to free SD space remotely (SPEC-R2-WORKSHOP-CAPTURE §6).

plugins.name: log-tcp
plugins.description: TCP fan-out of the device's log::info!/warn!/error! stream on port 21046. Multiple dashboard clients can `nc <ip> 21046` to tail the live log (SPEC-R2-WORKSHOP-SENSOR-LIVE-LOGS). Read-only; ignores any data sent by the client.

plugins.name: reset-tcp
plugins.description: TCP listener on port 21044 accepting a single CMD_RESET byte (0x52). On receipt, calls esp_restart(). Implements SPEC-R2-WORKSHOP-SENSOR-REMOTE-RESET; used by the dashboard's `r2.dash.cmd.reset` flow.

plugins.name: led
plugins.description: Status LED driver — abstracts over WS2812 (RMT-driven RGB on devkitc) and mono LEDC PWM (xiao GPIO21 + dfr1117 GPIO15). The plugin exposes a small FSM-state-to-colour-or-blink mapping; the consuming Status sentant calls set_state(<state>) and the plugin handles the per-carrier rendering.

plugins.name: adxl355
plugins.description: Analog Devices ADXL355 precision triaxial MEMS accelerometer over SPI. 20-bit native resolution, low-noise, SHM-grade. r2-workshop's primary sensing element on the ESP32-S3 carriers (DevKitC + XIAO); capability-paired with `lis2dh` (I²C, 10-bit) — same `ai.reality2.cap.accel.triaxial`, different bus + precision, swappable per R2-PLUGIN §10.

plugins.name: battery-adc
plugins.description: Single-channel ADC reading the LiPo cell voltage through a 100 kΩ/100 kΩ divider. Converts raw ADC counts → millivolts at the cell terminal; consuming sentants compute charge state from voltage curves. ESP32-family ADC1 (ADC2 is unusable while WiFi is active).

plugins.name: lis2dh
plugins.description: STMicroelectronics LIS2DH 3-axis MEMS accelerometer over I²C. Provides the generic triaxial-accel capability for r2-workshop's dfr1117 carrier (sourced via DFRobot SEN0224 Gravity I²C module). 10-bit data, ±2/4/8/16 g, ODR up to 5.3 kHz. Capability-aligned peer to adxl355 (SPI, 20-bit, higher precision) — same capability provided, different chip + bus per R2-PLUGIN §10 swap lever.

plugins.name: sd-card
plugins.description: FATFS-mounted microSD card on a shared SPI bus, plus the 20-byte fixed-record acceleration ring (r2-workshop CAPTURE §6). Provides durable sample storage + capture-file management + boot recovery (replay-on-reconnect). Two-CS sharing with the accel plugin: only one chip-select is asserted per transaction.

sentants.name: Accelerometer
sentants.description: Domain sentant. Binds the generic accel.triaxial capability (NOT a specific chip), reads samples at the configured ODR, applies the calibration offset baked into vars, stamps with clock.now_ms, emits r2.sensor.acceleration. The sensing-element swap lever (lis2dh vs adxl355) keeps this sentant chip-agnostic.

sentants.name: Battery
sentants.description: Polls the battery-adc plugin every 30 s and emits r2.sensor.battery. Also flips low-battery state to the LED + a power_state_changed event so Beacon updates the BLE AD.

sentants.name: Beacon
sentants.description: Drives the BLE R2-BEACON advertiser. Starts after Identity reaches ready (rbid populated); calls `ble-beacon` plugin to emit the 28-byte legacy AD with class hash + RBID + state. Updates the AD on power-state changes (low battery, OTA in progress).

sentants.name: Bootstrap
sentants.description: Owns the BLE L2CAP `#wifi_offer` reception. On a valid TG-signed offer, decodes the FrameHeader byte (R2-WIRE) + compact frame, verifies the TG signature using the baked-in TG public key, persists WiFi creds to NVS, receives the KeyHolder-signed DeviceCertificate, then triggers a reboot to pick up the new credentials. Per R2-BOOTSTRAP §4.

sentants.name: Capture
sentants.description: Owns the Idle / Calibrating / Recording state machine per SPEC-R2-WORKSHOP-CAPTURE §2. On r2.dash.capture.start, opens a named capture file on the SD card and starts duplicating every r2.sensor.acceleration to it. On r2.dash.capture.stop, closes the file. Event marks (r2.dash.capture.event_mark) are written to a sidecar file (§7.5). Calibration samples (r2.dash.cal.sample.req) handled in the Calibrating state.

sentants.name: Health
sentants.description: Watches for a stuck data source per SPEC-R2-WORKSHOP-SENSOR-HEALTH §6. Tracks the last-arrived sample timestamp; if no acceleration arrives for > 2 s while in real-data-source mode, flags data_source="sim" via a set_data_source event (Status sentant catches + propagates).

sentants.name: Identity
sentants.description: One-shot at boot. Loads or creates the device's persistent Ed25519 keypair + the persistent RBID (R2-FNV(device_pk)) from NVS, sets them in the hive context, then enters idle. Mirrors the R2-HIVE device-identity contract — every subsequent sentant (Bootstrap, Beacon, Uplink, …) consumes the keypair from the hive context. Compilable subset (R2-COMPILE §3.1).

sentants.name: Ota
sentants.description: Owns the OTA update flow. Subscribes r2.dash.fw.update; delegates to the core ota-tcp plugin (port 21043). On successful image swap, sets status to Ota (LED → white strobe) until reboot. The mark_app_valid step is the responsibility of the Uplink sentant (after the first frame round-trips post-reboot — SENSOR §12.2).

sentants.name: Presence
sentants.description: One-shot at boot: 5× UDP broadcast to 255.255.255.255:21044 carrying {rbid, ip}. Drives the controller's RBID-based bootstrap reconciliation — after a sensor reboot, the controller knows the device's new IP within a few hundred ms without waiting for the next BLE scan cycle.

sentants.name: Recorder
sentants.description: Writes every r2.sensor.acceleration record to the SD ring buffer (CAPTURE §6). On r2.dash.ack {through_seq}, frees ring records up to that sequence. Stores last-acked-seq in NVS so cold boots can resume catch-up from the right place.

sentants.name: Reset
sentants.description: Handles remote-reset requests. Two paths: r2.dash.reset over R2-WIRE (preferred), or a TCP byte 0x52 to port 21044 via the reset-tcp plugin (fallback when R2-WIRE is silent). Both lead to esp_restart() after a brief LED blink to signal pending reboot.

sentants.name: Status
sentants.description: Emits r2.sensor.status every 2 s — FSM state, data_source (real/sim), seq watermark, uptime. Drives the dashboard's virtual LED + diagnostics. Also sets the physical LED state via the led plugin on every FSM transition received.

sentants.name: Sync
sentants.description: Time-sync per SPEC-R2-WORKSHOP-TIMESYNC §2 (Cristian's algorithm). On r2.dash.sync_pulse, immediately emits r2.sensor.sync_pong. On r2.dash.set_clock_offset, writes the new offset to the clock plugin. Wall-clock accuracy ~5 ms (network-jitter-bound).

sentants.name: Uplink
sentants.description: Single TCP session to the controller (port 21042). Sends the announce frame on connect (with cert + device_pk + class_hash), then forwards subscribed events as R2-WIRE compact frames. Reconnects with exponential backoff.

sentants.name: WifiProv
sentants.description: One-shot at boot. Reads WiFi credentials from NVS (set by Bootstrap) or wifi_config.toml (dev fallback) and drives the WiFi STA singleton to associate. On failure (no creds, or association timeout), yields to Bootstrap (sets LED to Advertising and starts BLE listening).
